35	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
36	
37	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
38	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
39	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
40	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
41	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
42	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
43	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
44	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
45	L543–545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
46	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `•`. Two short
47	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
48	structure literal give the upgrade.
49	
50	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
51	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
52	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
53	in a second namespace block. -/
54	
55	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
56	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
57	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
58	L543–545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241–243) is defined by
59	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
60	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177–179) gives
61	additivity. -/
62	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
63	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
64	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
65	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
66	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
67	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
68	  ext
69	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
70	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
71	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
72	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
74	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
75	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ← Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
76	
77	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
78	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
79	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52–55)
80	in place of `add_hom`. -/
81	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul
82	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
83	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ}
84	    (r : R) (a : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
85	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a) = r • Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a := by
86	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
87	  ext
88	  rw [Abelian.Ext.smul_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
89	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
90	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a)) =
91	    r • Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a)
92	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
93	      ← Abelian.Ext.smul_hom]
94	
95	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (c): the `R`-linear upgrade of Mathlib's bare-`Equiv`
96	`Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n` to a `LinearEquiv`,
97	when `C` is `Linear R`-enriched abelian. Combines (a) and (b). The
98	`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540 declaration is
99	universe-polymorphic but only proves the underlying bijection; this wrapper
100	adds the algebraic structure preservation needed for downstream `LinearEquiv`
101	chaining (specifically, the iter-034 universe bridge
102	`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` below). -/
103	noncomputable def Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv
104	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
105	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} :
106	    Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ₗ[R] Abelian.Ext.{w'} X Y n where
107	  toFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a
108	  invFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w} a
109	  left_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.left_inv a
110	  right_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.right_inv a
111	  map_add' a b := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add a b
112	  map_smul' r a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul r a
113	
114	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
115	
116	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
117	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
118	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
119	`X ↦ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
120	i.e.\ to a presheaf `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
121	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
122	
123	The codomain is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` (not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
124	`Abelian.extFunctor n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
125	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
126	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
127	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
128	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
129	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
130	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
131	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
132	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
133	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
134	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
135	    (n : ℕ) :
136	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
137	  Functor.flip
138	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
139	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
140	
141	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
142	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
143	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
144	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
145	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
146	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
147	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
148	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
149	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
150	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
151	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
152	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
153	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
154	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
155	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
156	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
157	
158	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
159	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
160	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
161	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
162	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
163	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for
164	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf → HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
165	
166	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (...).obj (op S.X₃)` is the biproduct in
167	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
168	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
169	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
170	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
171	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `δ`, iter-019 the LES sequence
172	and exactness theorem. -/
173	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
174	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
175	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
176	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
177	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
178	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
179	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
180	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
181	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
182	  biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
183	              ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
184	
185	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
186	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
187	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
188	as a single map out of the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
189	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file
190	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L67–70) for
191	the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
192	
193	The negation `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` uses the
194	preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms; the sign is the standard
195	Mayer-Vietoris convention encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech
196	complex. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). -/
197	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
198	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
199	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
200	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
201	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
202	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
203	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
204	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
205	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) :=
206	  biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
207	              (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
208	
209	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-018): the third Mayer-Vietoris LES building
210	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the composition-is-zero lemma asserting
211	`HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0`. Direct mirror
212	of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod_fromBiprod`
213	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`,
214	L72–75) for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
215	
216	The proof unwinds via the biproduct universal property
217	(`biprod.lift_desc : biprod.lift a b ≫ biprod.desc c d = a ≫ c + b ≫ d`),
218	the preadditive negation (`Preadditive.comp_neg`), the addition-of-negation
219	arithmetic (`← sub_eq_add_neg`), the zero-iff-equal arithmetic
220	(`sub_eq_zero`), the contravariant functoriality of `cohomologyPresheaf`
221	(`← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`), and the Mayer-Vietoris square
222	factorisation `S.toSquare.fac` (which says `S.f₁₂ ≫ S.f₂₄ = S.f₁₃ ≫ S.f₃₄`).
223	Probe-confirmed proof (iter-018 plan-agent); the `simp only` set transfers
224	verbatim from Mathlib's `AddCommGrpCat`-flavored proof because every step
225	is value-category-agnostic.
226	
227	The `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` attribute generates the post-composition
228	form `… ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod … ≫ Z = 0 ≫ Z = 0` and registers the lemma
229	as a `simp` lemma; both are required for downstream Mayer-Vietoris LES
230	exactness arguments. -/
231	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
232	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod
233	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
234	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
235	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
236	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
237	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
238	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0 := by
239	  simp only [HModule'_toBiprod, HModule'_fromBiprod, biprod.lift_desc,
240	    Preadditive.comp_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_zero,
241	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, S.toSquare.fac]

243	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): `ModuleCat.free k`
244	is left adjoint to `forget (ModuleCat k)`. Mathlib's
245	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` provides the adjunction
246	`ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)` but does not register
247	the corresponding `IsLeftAdjoint` instance (whereas `AddCommGrpCat.free.IsLeftAdjoint`
248	is registered in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` L84). This
249	project-local instance fills the gap so that `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
250	acquires the necessary `PreservesColimit` instance, used by
251	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` below. -/
252	instance ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint
253	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint :=
254	  ⟨_, ⟨ModuleCat.adj k⟩⟩

256	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
257	pushout-of-free-sheaves analog of Mathlib's
258	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf`
259	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L154–160).
260	States that the image of the Mayer-Vietoris square `S` under the composite
261	`yoneda ⋙ Functor.whiskeringRight ⋅ (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J _`
262	is a pushout square in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. The proof transfers the
263	type-level pushout `S.isPushout` through `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
264	(which preserves pushouts because `(ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint`, registered
265	in `ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint` above) and adjusts via the canonical iso
266	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso`.
267	
268	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); the proof is verbatim from Mathlib L156–160
269	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. Used downstream in iter-020+ to
270	prove `Mono` / `Epi` / `Exact` instances on the iter-019 short complex
271	`HModule'_shortComplex`, and ultimately in the iter-022+ `δ` connecting hom. -/
272	lemma HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf
273	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
274	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
275	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
276	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
277	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
278	      presheafToSheaf J _)).IsPushout :=
279	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
280	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
281	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
282	        (S.map yoneda))

284	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
285	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
286	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
287	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234–249) for the
288	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
289	
290	The objects are the sheafified free `ModuleCat k`-valued presheaves on the four
291	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X₂` and `S.X₃`
292	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f₁₂)
293	– (yoneda.map S.f₁₃)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum
294	`(yoneda.map S.f₂₄) + (yoneda.map S.f₃₄)` desced through the biproduct).
295	The `zero` proof (i.e.\ `f ≫ g = 0`) follows from the
296	`cokernelCofork.condition` of the pushout square in
297	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` above.
298	
299	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); structure-literal mirror of Mathlib L235–249
300	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The `@[simps]` attribute generates
301	field-projection simp lemmas (`HModule'_shortComplex_X₁`, `..._X₂`, `..._X₃`,
302	`..._f`, `..._g`) consumed by the iter-020+ `Mono`/`Epi`/`Exact`/`ShortExact`
303	proofs and the iter-022+ `δ` definition. -/
304	@[simps]
305	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
306	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
307	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
308	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
309	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
310	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
311	  X₁ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₁ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
312	  X₂ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₂ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k) ⊞
313	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₃ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
314	  X₃ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₄ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
315	  f :=
316	    biprod.lift
317	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₂) _))
318	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₃) _))
319	  g :=
320	    biprod.desc
321	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₂₄) _))
322	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₃₄) _))
323	  zero :=
324	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
325	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition
326	
327	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): the free-module
328	functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` preserves monomorphisms.
329	Mathlib registers `AddCommGrpCat.instPreservesMonomorphismsFree :
330	AddCommGrpCat.free.PreservesMonomorphisms` in
331	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` but does not register the
332	corresponding instance for `ModuleCat.free k` in
333	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean`. This project-local
334	instance fills the gap so that, after `simp only [biprod.lift_snd]`, the
335	typeclass-search engine can discharge the residual `Mono` goal in the proof
336	of `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` below.
337	
338	The proof: for an injective function `f : X → Y` between types,
339	`(ModuleCat.free k).map f = ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.lmapDomain k k f)` (defeq),
340	and `Finsupp.mapDomain f` is injective on `Finsupp X k → Finsupp Y k` by
341	Mathlib's `Finsupp.mapDomain_injective`. Bridging via `ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective`
342	recovers `Mono` in `ModuleCat k`. -/
343	instance ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms
344	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).PreservesMonomorphisms := by
345	  refine ⟨fun {X Y} f hf ↦ ?_⟩
346	  have hf' : Function.Injective f := (CategoryTheory.mono_iff_injective f).mp hf
347	  rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
348	  exact Finsupp.mapDomain_injective hf'
349	
350	-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` is a
351	-- monomorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
352	-- `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L251–257 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
353	-- The `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` attribute is
354	-- required because the typeclass-search engine needs to unfold the `dsimp`-normal
355	-- form of `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` past structure-literal projection.
356	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
357	instance HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono
358	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
359	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
360	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
361	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
362	    Mono (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f := by
363	  have : Mono ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).f ≫ biprod.snd) := by
364	    dsimp
365	    simp only [biprod.lift_snd]
366	    infer_instance
367	  exact mono_of_mono _ biprod.snd
368	
369	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).g` is an
370	epimorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
371	`MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L259–261 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
372	The proof is a one-line term-mode body using
373	`ShortComplex.exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
374	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
375	instance HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi
376	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
377	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
378	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
379	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
380	    Epi (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g :=
381	  ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel.2
382	    ⟨(HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork⟩).2
383	
384	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact`,
385	i.e. the kernel of `g` equals the image of `f` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`.
386	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L263–265 with
387	`AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line term-mode
388	body using `ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
389	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
390	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_exact
391	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
392	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
393	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
394	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
395	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact :=
396	  ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel _
397	    (HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork

399	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact`,
400	the short-exact predicate (combining `Mono f`, `Epi g`, and `Exact`) in
401	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean`
402	L267–268 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line
403	anonymous-constructor: the `Mono f` and `Epi g` predicates are typeclass-resolved
404	from `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` and `HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi`, leaving
405	only the `exact` field which is filled with `HModule'_shortComplex_exact`. The
406	named lemma is consumed in iter-021+ as `S.HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact.extClass`
407	to define the connecting hom `HModule'_δ` of the Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
408	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact
409	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

412	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :

414	  exact := HModule'_shortComplex_exact k S

416	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-021): the connecting homomorphism `δ` of the
417	Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology.
418	For a Mayer-Vietoris square `S` and adjacent cohomological degrees `n₀ + 1 = n₁`,
419	the morphism
420	  δ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (op S.X₁) ⟶
421	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (op S.X₄)
422	in `AddCommGrpCat` is the precomposition with the extension class
423	`(HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass : Ext (...X₁...) (...X₃...) 1`
424	followed by `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` to wrap the resulting `AddMonoidHom` as a
425	categorical morphism. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.δ`
426	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L112–114)
427	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
428	
429	The `[HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` typeclass is required (the
430	`Ext.precomp` operation depends on it) and matches the corresponding
431	requirement on iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `..._cohomologyPresheaf`,
432	iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `..._fromBiprod`, and iter-018
433	`HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod`.
434	
435	This connecting hom is the missing third link of the Mayer-Vietoris exact
436	sequence: combined with iter-017's `toBiprod` (sum of restriction) and
437	`fromBiprod` (difference of restriction), iter-018's `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0`,
438	and the iter-022+ packaging into a `ComposableArrows`-form sequence + iter-023+
439	sequence-iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + iter-024+ exactness theorem, it
440	will yield the full LES `... → Hⁿ(X₄) → Hⁿ(X₂) ⊞ Hⁿ(X₃) → Hⁿ(X₁) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X₄) → ...`
441	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology. -/
442	noncomputable def HModule'_δ
443	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
444	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
445	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
446	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
447	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
448	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
449	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
450	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
451	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
452	
453	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-022): the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
454	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology, packaged as a `ComposableArrows` of
455	length 5 in `AddCommGrpCat`. The five composable morphisms are, in order,
456	`HModule'_toBiprod` at degree `n₀`, `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₀`, the
457	connecting hom `HModule'_δ` from degree `n₀` to degree `n₁`, `HModule'_toBiprod`
458	at degree `n₁`, and `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₁`. Direct mirror of
459	Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.sequence`
460	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L120–122)
461	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
462	
463	The `noncomputable abbrev` form is load-bearing: downstream `dsimp`-based
464	unfolding (in iter-023+ `HModule'_sequenceIso` and iter-024+
465	`HModule'_sequence_exact`) needs to access `mk₅`'s field-projection simp
466	lemmas through the abbrev. -/
467	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_sequence
468	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
469	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
470	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
471	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
472	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
473	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
474	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
475	  ComposableArrows.mk₅ (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₀) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀)
476	    (HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h)
477	    (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₁)

479	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 1: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_toBiprod`
480	(mirror Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L48–64). -/
481	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_apply
482	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
483	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
484	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
485	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
486	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
487	    {n : ℕ} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
488	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
489	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
490	        ⟨(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
491	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
492	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
493	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
494	  ext
495	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
496	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
497	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
498	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
499	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
500	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
501

503	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77–83). -/
504	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
505	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

508	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
509	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
510	    {n : ℕ}
511	    (y₁ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂))
512	    (y₂ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃)) :

516	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op y₂ := by
517	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]

519	  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]

521	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 3: bridges `AddCommGrpCat`-side `biprodIsoProd` and
522	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism

531	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
532	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))

534	    Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
535	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n x)) =

539	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 4: bridges the same machinery for the `fromBiprod`
540	-- morphism (mirror Mathlib L93–106).
541	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
542	attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
543	lemma HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
544	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
545	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
546	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
547	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
548	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
549	    {n : ℕ}
550	    (x : ↑((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
551	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃))) :
552	    (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f).comp
553	        (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm
554	          ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom x)) (zero_add n) =
555	      (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n x) := by
556	  obtain ⟨⟨x₂, x₃⟩, rfl⟩ :=
557	    (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.symm.surjective x

559	  rw [Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv_symm_apply,
560	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
561	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
562	  cat_disch
563	
564	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
565	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124–138). The technical heart
566	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
567	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
568	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
569	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
570	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
571	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
572	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
573	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
574	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
575	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
576	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
577	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
578	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
579	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
580	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
581	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
582	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
583	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
584	    (Iso.refl _)
585	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
586	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
587	    (by dsimp; rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]; rfl)
588	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
589	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
590	
591	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
592	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
593	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
594	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
595	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
596	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
597	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
598	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
599	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
600	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
601	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
602	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
603	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
604	
605	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
606	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
607	lemma HModule'_δ_toBiprod
608	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
609	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
610	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
611	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
612	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
613	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
614	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
615	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
616	
617	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
618	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
619	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
620	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
621	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
622	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
623	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
624	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
625	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
626	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
627	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1
628	
629	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCore
7	
8	/-!
9	# Mayer-Vietoris on a 2-affine cover and Čech-acyclic cover infrastructure
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 2) contains the 2-affine cover
12	Mayer-Vietoris theorems, the cover-totality bridges (universe-shift
13	`LinearEquiv`s, `finrank` / `Module.Finite` transports), the
14	`IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers, the comparison-iso typeclass
15	`HasCechToHModuleIso`, and the existence-bundled
16	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` predicate together with the producer that
17	fires `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from a `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`
18	hypothesis.
19	
20	This is cohort 2 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
21	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
22	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean` (this file): 2-affine cover MV +
23	  cover-totality bridges + `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers +
24	  `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
25	* `BasicOpenCech.lean`: basic-open cover wrappers + the substantive Čech
26	  acyclicity theorem.
27	
28	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u v w w'
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	section AffineCoverMVSquare
40	
41	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): a bundled 2-affine cover of a scheme
42	together with the affineness of the pairwise intersection. The geometric input
43	to the abstract Mayer-Vietoris LES `HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes — packaged
44	to record the affineness conditions and the cover-totality hypothesis that the
45	abstract MV-square does not capture. The accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`
46	produces the underlying Mathlib `MayerVietorisSquare`.
47	
48	Mathematically: a 2-affine open cover of `X` whose pairwise intersection is also
49	affine. The cover hypothesis `cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` is load-bearing for downstream
50	Serre-finiteness use (it identifies the `MayerVietorisSquare` corner `X₄` with
51	the whole scheme `X`). -/
52	structure AffineCoverMVSquare (X : Scheme.{u}) where
53	  /-- First affine open of the cover. -/
54	  U₁ : X.Opens
55	  /-- Second affine open of the cover. -/
56	  U₂ : X.Opens
57	  /-- `U₁` is affine. -/
58	  isAffineOpen_U₁ : IsAffineOpen U₁
59	  /-- `U₂` is affine. -/
60	  isAffineOpen_U₂ : IsAffineOpen U₂
61	  /-- The intersection `U₁ ⊓ U₂` is affine. -/
62	  isAffineOpen_inf : IsAffineOpen (U₁ ⊓ U₂)
63	  /-- Total cover: `U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤`. -/
64	  cover : U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤
65	
66	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-028): the underlying abstract Mayer-Vietoris
67	square produced by a 2-affine cover. Bridge from the geometric input (an
68	`AffineCoverMVSquare`) to the categorical input (a `MayerVietorisSquare` for
69	the Grothendieck topology on `Opens X.toTopCat`) the abstract MV-LES theorem
70	`HModule'_sequence_exact` consumes. The four corners collapse definitionally:
71	`X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`, `X₂ = U₁`, `X₃ = U₂`, `X₄ = U₁ ⊔ U₂` (each by `rfl`,
72	plan-agent probe-verified). -/
73	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare {X : Scheme.{u}}
74	    (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
75	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat).MayerVietorisSquare :=
76	  Opens.mayerVietorisSquare S.U₁ S.U₂
77	
78	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₁ = U₁ ⊓ U₂`. The first three corners are
79	`rfl`-equal because `Opens.mayerVietorisSquare` defines them definitionally. -/
80	@[simp]
81	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₁
82	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
83	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₁ = S.U₁ ⊓ S.U₂ := rfl
84	
85	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₂ = U₁`. -/
86	@[simp]
87	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₂
88	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
89	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₂ = S.U₁ := rfl
90	
91	/-- Iter-029 corner identification: `X₃ = U₂`. -/
92	@[simp]
93	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₃
94	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
95	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₃ = S.U₂ := rfl
96	
97	/-- Iter-029 cover-totality identification: `X₄ = ⊤`. The substantive corner
98	identification — uses the `cover` field, which is the totality hypothesis
99	`U₁ ⊔ U₂ = ⊤` recorded in `AffineCoverMVSquare`. -/
100	@[simp]
101	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄
102	    {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare) :
103	    S.toMayerVietorisSquare.toSquare.X₄ = ⊤ := S.cover
104	
105	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES sequence on a 2-affine cover: a routine
106	specialisation of the iter-022 abstract sequence `HModule'_sequence` to the
107	bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. Parameterised over a generic sheaf `F`
108	for reusability; the `toModuleKSheaf C` application is iter-030+ work. The
109	fully-qualified `_root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence` reference
110	is required because `def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` auto-opens the
111	`AffineCoverMVSquare` namespace inside the body, causing bare
112	`HModule'_sequence` to recursively resolve to itself. -/
113	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence
114	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
115	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
116	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
117	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
118	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n₀ n₁ h
119	
120	/-- Iter-029 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover: a routine
121	specialisation of the iter-026 abstract exactness theorem
122	`HModule'_sequence_exact` to the bundled `AffineCoverMVSquare` data. -/
123	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_exact
124	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
125	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
126	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
127	    (S.HModule'_sequence k F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
128	  _root_.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_sequence_exact k S.toMayerVietorisSquare F n₀ n₁ h
129	
130	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES on a 2-affine cover of a curve: a routine
131	binding of the iter-029 sheaf-parameterised `HModule'_sequence` to the
132	structure sheaf `toModuleKSheaf C` for `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`.
133	The dot-notation `S.HModule'_sequence` resolves cleanly via method-call
134	against `S : AffineCoverMVSquare`, sidestepping the iter-029 sub-namespace
135	shadowing trap that required `_root_` disambiguation in the abstract
136	specialisation's body. -/
137	noncomputable def AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve
138	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
139	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
140	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
141	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
142	  S.HModule'_sequence k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n₀ n₁ h
143	
144	/-- Iter-030 Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness on a 2-affine cover of a curve:
145	the exactness companion of `HModule'_sequence_curve`, by direct application
146	of the iter-029 abstract `HModule'_sequence_exact` to the structure sheaf
147	`toModuleKSheaf C`. -/
148	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve_exact
149	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
150	    (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
151	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
152	    (S.HModule'_sequence_curve k n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
153	  S.HModule'_sequence_exact k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n₀ n₁ h
154	
155	end AffineCoverMVSquare
156	
157	/-- Iter-031 cover-totality source-object identification. For a category `C`
158	with terminal object `T`, the sheafification of the free `k`-module on the
159	representable presheaf at `T` is canonically isomorphic to the constant sheaf
160	at `ModuleCat.of k k`. This is the natural-iso prerequisite for identifying
161	`HModule' k F n T` (open-evaluation cohomology at the terminal `T`, iter-014)
162	with `HModule k F n` (global cohomology, iter-009): both are `Ext` groups
163	whose source objects are made canonically isomorphic by this iso.
164	
165	Assembled from three Mathlib pieces, mirroring the structure
166	`constantSheaf J D = Functor.const Cᵒᵖ ⋙ presheafToSheaf J D`:
167	1. `yoneda.obj T ≅ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit` — the terminal-collapse
168	   of the representable, by `IsTerminal.from`/`IsTerminal.hom_ext`.
169	2. `Functor.constComp _ PUnit (ModuleCat.free k)` — the identity
170	   `(Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj PUnit ⋙ ModuleCat.free k =
171	    (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj ((ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit)`
172	   packaged as an iso.
173	3. `(Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso` —
174	   `(ModuleCat.free k).obj PUnit ≅ ModuleCat.of k k`. -/
175	noncomputable def HModule'_top_sourceIso
176	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
177	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
178	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
179	    {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
180	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj
181	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)
182	      ≅ (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) :=
183	  (presheafToSheaf J _).mapIso
184	    (Functor.isoWhiskerRight
185	        (NatIso.ofComponents
186	          (fun _ => Equiv.toIso { toFun := fun _ => PUnit.unit
187	                                  invFun := fun _ => hT.from _
188	                                  left_inv := fun _ => hT.hom_ext _ _
189	                                  right_inv := fun _ => rfl })
190	          (fun _ => by ext; rfl))
191	        (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
192	      Functor.constComp _ PUnit.{u+1} (ModuleCat.free k) ≪≫
193	      (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).mapIso
194	        (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique k k PUnit).toModuleIso)
195	
196	/-- Iter-032 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1`. Combining the
197	iter-031 `HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source
198	objects of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
199	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element) yields
200	a `k`-linear equivalence between the universe-`u+1` Ext at the sheafified
201	representable on `T` and `HModule k F n`.
202	
203	The universe annotation `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}` on the LHS is load-bearing —
204	without it Lean would pick `Ext.{u}` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` choice),
205	and the equivalence would not typecheck against `HModule k F n : Type (u+1)`
206	(iter-009). By pinning the LHS to `Ext.{u+1}` we sidestep the iter-031
207	universe mismatch (`HModule' : Type u` vs `HModule : Type (u+1)`); both
208	sides of this equivalence live at `Type (u+1)`. The bridge from
209	`HModule' k F n T : Type u` to the LHS here is iter-033+ work — most
210	plausibly via a small refactor on iter-014 to align `HModule'`'s universe,
211	or via a `ULift.{u+1}` wrapper.
212	
213	Body uses `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two pre-composition maps via the
214	iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip equations close by a four-step
215	rewrite: associativity (with the middle factor degree 0), composition
216	collapse via `mk₀_comp_mk₀`, the iso identity, and `mk₀_id_comp`. -/
217	noncomputable def HModule_top_linearEquiv
218	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
219	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
220	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
221	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
222	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
223	    Abelian.Ext.{u+1}
224	        ((presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
225	          ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj T)) F n
226	      ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n :=
227	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
228	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
229	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
230	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
231	    (by
232	      ext x
233	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
234	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
235	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
236	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
237	    (by
238	      ext y
239	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
240	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
241	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
242	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
243	
244	/-- Iter-033 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`. The universe-`u`
245	parallel of iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`. Combining the iter-031
246	`HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source objects
247	of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
248	`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element)
249	yields a `k`-linear equivalence between `HModule' k F n T` (the iter-014
250	universe-`u` cohomology of an open at the terminal) and the universe-`u`
251	`Ext` at the constant sheaf on `ModuleCat.of k k`.
252	
253	Both sides live at `Type u` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` ascription).
254	This is the universe-`u` parallel of iter-032's universe-`u+1` form; the
255	final bridge between the two (`Ext.{u}((constantSheaf J _).obj
256	(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n = Ext.{u+1}(...)`) is
257	iter-034+ work, requiring Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` (a bare
258	`Equiv`, file `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
259	L540) to be upgraded to a `LinearEquiv`.
260	
261	The body is shape-identical to iter-032: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two
262	pre-composition maps via the iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip
263	equations close by the same four-step rewrite chain. Lean's universe
264	inference adapts automatically because the LHS `HModule' k F n T`
265	forces `Ext.{u}` everywhere. -/
266	noncomputable def HModule'_top_linearEquiv
267	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
268	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
269	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
270	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
271	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
272	    HModule' k F n T
273	      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
274	              (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
275	  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
276	  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
277	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
278	    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
279	    (by
280	      ext x
281	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
282	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
283	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
284	          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
285	    (by
286	      ext y
287	      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
288	        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
289	      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
290	          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
291	
292	/-- Iter-034 universe-bump bridge. Composes iter-033's
293	`HModule'_top_linearEquiv` (universe-`u` cover-totality between `HModule' k F n T`
294	and `Ext.{u} ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`) with the
295	universe shift `Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (Mathlib gap-fill at the top
296	of this file) lifting the result to `Ext.{u+1} ((constantSheaf J _).obj
297	(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n = HModule k F n` (definitional unfold of the iter-009
298	abbrev). The composition gives the full bridge `HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k]
299	HModule k F n` for terminal `T`, closing Step 3 of the Serre-finiteness
300	scaffold. Iter-035+ specialises this to `AffineCoverMVSquare` using
301	iter-029's `toMayerVietorisSquare_toSquare_X₄ : ... = ⊤` simp lemma. -/
302	noncomputable def HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv
303	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

605	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
606	    Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
607	  subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
608	
609	/-- Iter-052: generalised rewrite-bridge HModule' consumer (abstract sheaf form).
610	Combines iter-051's `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso` with a
611	`▸`-style rewrite via the equality hypothesis `h : ⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U`: under both
612	class hypotheses (and the equality), conclude `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n U)`
613	on an arbitrary open `U`.
614	
615	Body: applies iter-051's `(⨆𝒰)`-form consumer to obtain `Subsingleton
616	(HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))`, then rewrites `⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U` via `h ▸ this` to
617	land on `U`.
618	
619	This is the iter-052 generalisation of iter-051: iter-051 covers the
620	`(⨆𝒰)`-form directly; iter-052 covers an arbitrary `U` under the equality
621	hypothesis. The iter-054+ producer for `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` chains: pick
622	a basic-open cover `𝒰` of an affine open `U` with `⨆𝒰 = U`, instantiate
623	`[IsCechAcyclicCover]` + `[HasCechToHModuleIso]`, apply iter-052 (this iteration)
624	with `h := the_cover_eq_U` to land `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` directly.
625	
626	Mirrors iter-049's `(h : ⨆𝒰 = ⊤)`-style rewrite at universe `u+1` for
627	`HModule`; iter-052 is the parallel form at universe `u` for `HModule'`. Stays
628	single-universe `[HasExt.{u}]` (no iter-034 universe bridge needed). -/
629	theorem subsingleton_HModule'_of_hasCechToHModuleIso
630	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
631	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
632	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
633	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
634	    [IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰] [HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]
635	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
636	    (h : ⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
637	    Subsingleton (HModule' k F n U) := by
638	  haveI := subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
639	  exact h ▸ this
640	
641	/-- Iter-052: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
642	
643	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 / iter-048 / iter-049 / iter-050 /
644	iter-051 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in
645	the curve setting (where `F` is the structure sheaf) from re-typing
646	`toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-052 generalised rewrite-bridge consumer is
647	chained through. Used by the iter-054+ producer instance for
648	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
649	theorem subsingleton_HModule'_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve
650	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
651	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
652	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
653	    [IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
654	    [HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
655	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
656	    (h : ⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
657	    Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) n U) :=
658	  subsingleton_HModule'_of_hasCechToHModuleIso (𝒰 := 𝒰) h n hn
659	
660	/-- Iter-053: existence-bundled affine Čech-acyclic + comparison-iso cover predicate.
661	
662	For each affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat`, asserts the existence of a
663	(data-bundled) basic-open cover `𝒰 : ι → Opens` with `⨆𝒰 = U` together with
664	both `IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰` (iter-048) and `HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰` (iter-050)
665	class hypotheses.
666	
667	Iter-053 is a thin scaffolding step — the existence is asserted, not constructed.
668	The substantive iter-054+ work will instantiate `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` for
669	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` via the standard Koszul + Čech-derived
670	comparison route (Hartshorne III.4 / Stacks 03F7 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra
671	§17 Koszul resolution).
672	
673	Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 / iter-048 / iter-050 carrier-predicate pattern:
674	package the substantive multi-iteration work behind a `Prop`-class so that
675	downstream consumers (iter-053 producer + iter-055+ Module.Finite ladder) can
676	fire via instance synthesis. -/
677	class HasAffineCechAcyclicCover
678	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
679	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
680	    Prop where
681	  exists_cover : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
682	    IsAffineOpen U →
683	      ∃ (ι : Type u) (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat),
684	        (⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U) ∧ IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰 ∧ HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰
685	
686	/-- Iter-053: producer `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` from `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover`.
687	
688	Under the iter-053 carrier hypothesis, conclude `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`
689	on every affine open `U` and every degree `i > 0`. The proof unbundles the
690	existential to obtain `ι, 𝒰, h, [IsCechAcyclicCover], [HasCechToHModuleIso]`,
691	registers the two instances locally with `haveI`, then chains iter-052's
692	generalised rewrite-bridge consumer `subsingleton_HModule'_of_hasCechToHModuleIso`
693	to land `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)`.
694	
695	Closes the bridge from existence-of-acyclic-cover (iter-053 carrier; substantive
696	iter-054+ work fills it for the structure sheaf) to per-affine-open vanishing
697	(iter-040 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`). Once the iter-054+ producer instance for
698	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` is supplied, this producer
699	fires automatically, supplying `[IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)]`
700	to iter-040's consumer chain and unblocking Phase A step 6. -/
701	instance instIsAffineHModuleVanishing_of_hasAffineCechAcyclicCover
702	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
703	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
704	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
705	    [HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F] : IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F where
706	  subsingleton_HModule' {U} hU i hi := by
707	    obtain ⟨ι, 𝒰, hcov, hacyc, hcomp⟩ :=
708	      HasAffineCechAcyclicCover.exists_cover (F := F) hU
709	    haveI : IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰 := hacyc
710	    haveI : HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰 := hcomp
711	    exact subsingleton_HModule'_of_hasCechToHModuleIso hcov i hi
712	
713	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: sheafification, Ext, and the structure sheaf
10	
11	Phase A step 5 (per `STRATEGY.md`):
12	
13	* **Prerequisites** (iter-005, closed): the `HasSheafify` and `HasExt` instances
14	  on the topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space, valued in
15	  `ModuleCat k`. The `Linear k` enrichment on the resulting sheaf category is
16	  auto-inferable from Mathlib and therefore needs no scaffold here; together
17	  with `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule` it gives the path to a
18	  `Module k` structure on `Ext` groups.
19	* **Main** (iter-006, scaffolded here): the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme
20	  `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` viewed as a sheaf of `k`-modules. Built
21	  from the structure-morphism-derived ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` (helpers (1)–(5))
22	  and packaged as the presheaf `toModuleKPresheaf` (6), proven to be a sheaf
23	  by `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (7), and bundled as `toModuleKSheaf` (8).
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 006 — refactor scaffold)
28	
29	The two prerequisite declarations (lines below) are honestly closed (iter-005).
30	The eight Phase A step 5 main declarations are scaffolded as `sorry`. The
31	iter-006 prover round is responsible for filling them.
32	-/
33	
34	set_option autoImplicit false
35	
36	universe u v
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
39	
40	namespace CategoryTheory
41	
42	universe u₂ v₂
43	
44	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
45	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
46	`ext; rfl`. -/
47	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
48	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
49	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
50	
51	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
52	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
53	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
54	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
55	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
56	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
57	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
58	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
59	
60	namespace Adjunction
61	
62	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
63	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
64	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
65	
66	include adj
67	
68	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
69	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
70	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
71	default simp lemmas. -/
72	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
73	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
74	    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])
75	
76	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
77	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
78	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
79	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
80	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
81	
82	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
83	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
84	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
85	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
86	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
87	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
88	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
89	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
90	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
91	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
92	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
93	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
94	      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }
95	
96	end Adjunction
97	
98	end CategoryTheory

125	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf

127	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
128	
129	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
130	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
131	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
132	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
133	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
134	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
135	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
136	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
137	
138	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
139	map of `kToSection`. -/
140	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
142	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
143	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
146	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
147	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
148	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
149	  rfl
150	
151	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
152	natural in `U`. -/
153	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
154	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
155	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
156	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
157	  rfl
158	
159	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
160	`algebraMap`. -/
161	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
162	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
163	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
164	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
165	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
166	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
167	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
168	
169	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf

173	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
174	
175	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
176	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
177	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
178	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
179	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
180	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
181	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
182	      map_add' := fun x y => by
183	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
184	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
185	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
186	        congr 1
187	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
188	          (C := C) f r }
189	  map_id U := by
190	    ext x
191	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
192	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
193	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
194	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
195	    ext x
196	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
197	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
198	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
199	
200	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true

200	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
201	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
202	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
203	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
204	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
205	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
206	  rfl
207	
208	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
209	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
210	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
211	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
212	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
213	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
214	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
215	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
216	
217	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
218	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
219	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
220	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
221	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
222	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
223	
224	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
225	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
226	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
227	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
228	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
229	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
230	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
231	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
232	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
233	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
234	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
235	  Iso.refl _
236	
237	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
238	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
239	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
240	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
241	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
242	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
243	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
244	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
245	noncomputable abbrev HModule
246	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
247	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
248	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
249	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
250	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
251	
252	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
253	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
254	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
255	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
256	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
257	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
258	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
259	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
260	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
261	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a

525	from the geometric content of $C$.

539	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
540	vanishing) instead of iter-041's. -/

546	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)
